hero.headline: Building Ventures on AI. No Big Team. Just Agents.
hero.subheadline: The Unnamed Roads is an AI venture studio. 9 live projects. Built by one founder and a stack of AI agents.
hero.supportingLine: 📎 Stack: n8n · OpenClaw · LiteLLM · Coolify · Hetzner

ventures.title: Nine projects, all running on agents
ventures.description: Each project publishes AI-generated content daily, tracks analytics via Umami, and notifies via Telegram. No manual editing. No CMS logins.

companies.name: THE UNNAMED ROADS
companies.summary: The studio hub. Publishes articles on AI infrastructure, solo founder ops, and self-hosted systems.

companies.name: THE HOCKEY ANALYTICS
companies.summary: AI-generated NHL analysis, standings explainers, and xG/Corsi breakdowns. Published daily via n8n + Groq.

companies.name: THE HOCKEY BRAIN
companies.summary: Plain-language hockey stats guides targeting casual fans. Gemini-powered with live NHL API data.

companies.name: THE AGENT FABRIC
companies.summary: B2B content site on distributed AI agents, edge deployments, and sovereign AI. Built with Next.js.

companies.name: THE ATOMIC NETWORK
companies.summary: Landing page for a payment micro-network concept. Netlify-hosted, content published via OpenClaw.

companies.name: THE PRINT ROUTE
companies.summary: Landing page for an automated print order routing platform. React/Vite, deployed on Vercel.

companies.name: EMILINGEMARKARLSSON.COM
companies.summary: Personal portfolio and blog. AI-generated SEO articles, Astro-based, deployed on Netlify.

aiTeams.title: The agent pipeline
aiTeams.subtitle: Three layers handle everything: research, content generation, and publishing. Each runs on a schedule with Telegram notifications and zero manual steps.
aiTeams.note: One human sets strategy, approves sensitive changes, and checks Telegram. The rest is autonomous.

groups.name: Research Layer
groups.description: Keyword research, NHL API data pulls, and topic scoring run every morning before content generation.

members.name: n8n
members.bio: Self-hosted on Coolify. Runs ~23 active workflows across all 7 sites — cron-triggered, chained, with error monitoring.

members.name: Groq API
members.bio: Scores keyword candidates by relevance and competition, then generates full article drafts. Llama 3 models.

members.name: NHL API
members.strengths: Real-time standings, game scores, player stats
members.bio: Powers The Hockey Analytics and The Hockey Brain with current season data — no manual data entry.

groups.name: Content Layer
groups.description: Generation, formatting, and quality scoring before anything is published.

members.name: LiteLLM
members.bio: Self-hosted LLM gateway that routes requests to Groq, Gemini, or other providers. Tracks spend per site.

members.name: Gemini API
members.bio: Used for The Hockey Brain's daily NHL articles — longer format with richer context than Groq.

members.name: Minio
members.bio: Stores keyword JSONs, approved article drafts, and performance insight files between pipeline stages.

groups.name: Publish Layer
groups.description: From draft to live in seconds — GitHub commits, IndexNow pings, newsletter dispatch, and Telegram confirmations.

members.name: OpenClaw
members.strengths: Markdown write + GitHub commit in one call
members.bio: Webhook server on the host machine. Receives article payloads, writes markdown files, commits to GitHub repos, and triggers Netlify/Vercel deploys.

members.name: GitHub
members.bio: All site content lives in GitHub repos. Every commit triggers an automatic build and deploy.

members.name: Telegram Bot
members.bio: Every workflow sends a Telegram message on completion. EIK articles require inline button approval before publishing.

signals.title: Agents as the only workforce
signals.description: Building a fully automated company where AI agents handle every operational role — on a cost-optimized platform designed for maximum profitability with zero headcount.

highlights.label: Agents as the only employees
highlights.detail: Every operational role — content, research, publishing, analytics, notifications — is handled by AI agents running on schedule. No team. No overhead.

highlights.label: Cost-optimized for profitability
highlights.detail: Running 9 live projects with daily AI content for ~400 kr/month. Coolify + Hetzner replaces 2 300+ kr/month in managed services — maximizing margin from day one.

highlights.label: Fully automated publishing loops
highlights.detail: Research → draft → quality score → publish → IndexNow → newsletter — end-to-end autonomous. One human sets strategy. Agents execute everything else.

operatingSystem.title: How a site publishes without me touching it
operatingSystem.description: Every content site runs the same loop — scheduled by n8n, generated by an LLM, committed to GitHub by OpenClaw, deployed by Netlify or Vercel.

phases.title: Keyword research
phases.description: n8n reads yesterday's performance data from Minio. Groq scores topic candidates against real traffic signals.

phases.title: Article generation
phases.description: LiteLLM routes the prompt to Groq or Gemini. Article is scored for quality before continuing.

phases.title: Approve or auto-publish
phases.description: THA and THB auto-publish. EIK articles send a Telegram message with inline Approve/Reject buttons.

phases.title: Commit to GitHub
phases.description: OpenClaw writes the markdown file and commits to the site repo. Netlify or Vercel picks up the push and deploys in ~60 seconds.

phases.title: IndexNow + newsletter
phases.description: IndexNow pings Google and Bing with the new URL. Newsletter dispatch fires to Listmonk subscribers.

testimonials.title: What the infra actually costs
testimonials.description: Full monthly breakdown for running 7 sites with daily AI content publishing.

quotes.quote: One VPS running Coolify, n8n, OpenClaw, LiteLLM, Minio, Umami, and Qdrant. Everything self-hosted.
quotes.author: Hetzner CX32

quotes.quote: Groq free tier covers most article generation. Gemini API for THB. LiteLLM tracks spend per site.
quotes.author: LLM APIs (Groq + Gemini)

quotes.quote: theunnamedroads.com, thehockeyanalytics.com, thehockeybrain.com — plus Netlify/Vercel free tiers.
quotes.author: Domains + hosting

tools.title: Tools we deploy daily
tools.description: Every OS phase has named tools so partners can see exactly how we sense, compose, deploy, and audit autonomous ventures.

groups.title: Self-hosted infrastructure
groups.description: Everything runs on one Hetzner VPS managed by Coolify. No managed databases, no SaaS overhead.

tools.name: Coolify
tools.description: Self-hosted PaaS that manages all containers, env vars, and deploys on the Hetzner VPS.

tools.name: Hetzner
tools.description: CX32 VPS in Frankfurt. ~150 kr/month. Runs everything — n8n, LiteLLM, Minio, Umami, OpenClaw, Qdrant.

tools.name: Minio
tools.description: S3-compatible object storage for draft files, keyword JSONs, and performance insight data.

groups.title: Workflow automation
groups.description: n8n runs 23 active workflows. Every schedule, API call, conditional branch, and Telegram notification.

tools.name: n8n
tools.description: Self-hosted workflow engine. Handles keyword research, content generation, publishing, analytics, and error monitoring.

tools.name: Telegram
tools.description: All notifications and approvals arrive here. Inline buttons for approving EIK articles without opening a browser.

tools.name: OpenClaw
tools.description: Webhook publish server. Receives article payloads and commits markdown to GitHub — one HTTP call per publish.

groups.title: LLM stack
groups.description: LiteLLM proxies all model calls. Groq handles speed, Gemini handles quality, costs tracked per site.

tools.name: LiteLLM
tools.description: Self-hosted LLM gateway. Routes to Groq, Gemini, or other providers. Tracks spend. Single endpoint for all models.

tools.name: Groq
tools.description: Fast inference for keyword scoring and article generation. Llama 3 models. Free tier covers most daily usage.

tools.name: Qdrant
tools.description: Self-hosted vector DB for semantic search and RAG experiments on article archives.

groups.title: Analytics + observability
groups.description: Umami tracks traffic across all 7 sites. Daily Telegram reports with week-over-week comparisons.

tools.name: Umami
tools.description: Privacy-first analytics. Self-hosted. Daily automated reports sent to Telegram with pageviews, referrers, and top articles.

tools.name: IndexNow
tools.description: Instant URL submission to Google and Bing after every article publish. No waiting for crawlers.

tools.name: Listmonk
tools.description: Self-hosted newsletter platform. Subscriber list synced with Resend for transactional delivery.

about.title: The Unnamed Roads
about.subtitle: An AI venture studio — one founder, 9 live projects, self-hosted AI infrastructure.
body: The Unnamed Roads is an AI venture studio — one person, nine live projects, and an AI agent pipeline that handles everything from keyword research to publishing.

The entire stack is self-hosted on a single Hetzner VPS managed by Coolify. n8n orchestrates ~23 workflows. LiteLLM routes LLM calls to Groq and Gemini. OpenClaw commits markdown to GitHub. Netlify and Vercel deploy. Total cost: ~400 kr/month.
This site is where I write about what I'm building, what breaks, and what it actually costs to run AI infrastructure at this scale. No fluff. Just systems.

pillars.title: Fully self-hosted
pillars.detail: Coolify + Hetzner replaces 2 300+ kr/month in managed SaaS. One VPS runs everything.

pillars.title: Autonomous publishing
pillars.detail: Content research, generation, and publishing run on cron schedules without manual steps.

pillars.title: Telegram-first ops
pillars.detail: Every workflow completion sends a Telegram message. Approvals use inline buttons. No dashboards needed.

contact.title: Questions about the stack?
contact.description: If you're building something similar — self-hosted AI infra, multi-site content ops, n8n workflows — I'm happy to talk. No sales pitch, just notes from the field.
